sentence: O ceo non está claro hoxe.
ipa: ˈo ˈθeo ˈnoŋ eˈsta ˈklaɾo ˈoʃe
gloss_en: The sky is not clear today.
notes: Distinción ⟨ce⟩ [θ] 'ceo' (Galician keeps /θ/, no seseo in the RAG standard) per regueira1996; word-final ⟨n⟩ velar [ŋ] 'non' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: Estes zapatos custan corenta euros.
ipa: ˈestes θaˈpatos ˈkustaŋ koˈɾenta ˈewɾos
gloss_en: These shoes cost forty euros.
notes: ⟨z⟩ [θ] 'zapatos' per regueira1996; velar [ŋ] in coda 'custan' and sandhi ⟨s⟩+voiced onset 'estes zapatos' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: A xuntanza é mañá ás tres.
ipa: ˈa ʃuˈntanθa ˈɛ maˈɲa ˈas ˈtɾes
gloss_en: The gathering is tomorrow at three.
notes: Open-mid [ɛ] in the copula 'é' (7-vowel system) per regueira1996; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'mañá' and prehistoric ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'xuntanza' per carballo_calero1979.

sentence: Meu irmán vive preto da vila.
ipa: ˈmew iˈɾmaŋ ˈbibe ˈpɾeto ˈda ˈbila
gloss_en: My brother lives near the town.
notes: Word-final ⟨n⟩ velar [ŋ] 'irmán' per freixeiro_mato1998; falling diphthong ⟨eu⟩ [ew] 'meu' per regueira1996.

sentence: Vai de fronte e torce pola primeira rúa.
ipa: ˈbaj ˈde ˈfɾonte ˈe ˈtoɾθe ˈpola pɾiˈmejɾa ˈrua
gloss_en: Go straight ahead and turn down the first street.
notes: No Romance diphthongisation: 'fronte','torce' keep the mid vowel (contrast Castilian fuente/tuerce) per regueira1996; word-initial trill [r] 'rúa' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: A caldeirada daquel sitio estaba moi boa.
ipa: ˈa kaldeˈjɾaða daˈkel ˈsitjo eˈstaβa ˈmoj ˈboa
gloss_en: The fish stew at that place was very good.
notes: Regional lexicon 'caldeirada' and gheada absent in the RAG standard (/g/ stays [ɡ]) per regueira1996; hiatus 'sitio estaba' resolved by sandhi per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: Onde está a chave do coche?
ipa: ˈonde eˈsta ˈa ˈtʃabe ˈdo ˈkotʃe
gloss_en: Where is the car key?
notes: Affricate ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'chave','coche' (no deaffrication in the standard) per regueira1996; vowel hiatus 'está a' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: Cantas persoas veñen cear?
ipa: ˈkantas ˈpeɾsoas ˈbeɲeŋ ˈθeaɾ
gloss_en: How many people are coming to dinner?
notes: Palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'veñen' with velar [ŋ] in its coda, and distinción ⟨ce⟩ [θ] 'cear' per regueira1996; hiatus 'persoas' per carballo_calero1979.

sentence: Se eu puidese, mercaría aquela casa.
ipa: ˈse ˈew pwiˈðese meɾkaˈɾia aˈkela ˈkasa
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that house.
notes: Regional verb 'mercar' (to buy) per carballo_calero1979; vowel hiatus 'se eu' resolved across the junction per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: Onte merquei cinco quilos de cereixas na praza.
ipa: ˈonte ˈmeɾkej ˈθinko ˈkilos ˈde θeˈɾejʃas ˈna ˈpɾaθa
gloss_en: Yesterday I bought five kilos of cherries in the square.
notes: Distinción ⟨c/z⟩ [θ] 'cinco','cereixas','praza' per regueira1996; ⟨ei⟩+⟨x⟩ [ejʃ] 'cereixas' per carballo_calero1979.

sentence: O ano que vén hei viaxar a Compostela.
ipa: ˈo ˈano ˈke ˈbɛŋ ˈej bjaˈʃaɾ ˈa kompoˈstela
gloss_en: Next year I will travel to Compostela.
notes: Open-mid [ɛ] under a velar coda 'vén' [bɛŋ] per regueira1996; ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'viaxar' per carballo_calero1979.

sentence: Chámame cando chegues á casa.
ipa: ˈtʃamame ˈkando ˈtʃeɡes ˈa ˈkasa
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: Affricate ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'chámame','chegues' per regueira1996; ⟨gu⟩ [ɡ] before front vowel 'chegues' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: Boas tardes, como che vai a vida?
ipa: ˈboas ˈtaɾdes ˈkomo ˈtʃe ˈbaj ˈa ˈbiða
gloss_en: Good afternoon, how is life treating you?
notes: Clitic ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'che' (dative 2sg) per carballo_calero1979; hiatus 'vai a' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: Máis vale paxaro na man que cento voando.
ipa: ˈmais ˈbale paˈʃaɾo ˈna ˈmaŋ ˈke ˈθento ˈboando
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'paxaro' and distinción ⟨ce⟩ [θ] 'cento' per regueira1996; word-final velar [ŋ] 'man' and intervocalic -n- loss 'voando' (< voando) per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: O can corre pola ribeira do río.
ipa: ˈo ˈkaŋ ˈkore ˈpola riˈβejɾa ˈdo ˈrio
gloss_en: The dog runs along the riverbank.
notes: Word-final velar [ŋ] 'can' per freixeiro_mato1998; trill/tap contrast ⟨rr⟩ [r] 'corre' and word-initial [r] 'río' vs tap [ɾ] 'ribeira' per regueira1996.

sentence: A muller vella colle a agulla amarela.
ipa: ˈa muˈʎeɾ ˈbeʎa ˈkoʎe ˈa ˈaɡʎa amaˈɾela
gloss_en: The old woman picks up the yellow needle.
notes: Palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'muller','vella','colle','agulla' — Galician resists yeísmo and keeps the lateral per regueira1996; hiatus 'colle a agulla' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: O café da esquina é moi forte.
ipa: ˈo kaˈfɛ ˈda eˈskina ˈɛ ˈmoj ˈfoɾte
gloss_en: The coffee from the corner is very strong.
notes: Two open-mid [ɛ] nuclei 'café','é' witnessing the 7-vowel contrast per regueira1996; hiatus 'esquina é' per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: A viña da montaña dá bo viño.
ipa: ˈa ˈbiɲa ˈda moˈntaɲa ˈda ˈbo ˈbiɲo
gloss_en: The vineyard on the mountain gives good wine.
notes: Palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'viña','montaña','viño' per carballo_calero1979; regional apocope 'bo' (< bon) per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: Unha das mulleres colleu algunha flor.
ipa: ˈuŋa ˈdas muˈʎeɾes ˈkoʎew ˈalɡŋa ˈfloɾ
gloss_en: One of the women picked some flower.
notes: Velar nasal spelled ⟨nh⟩ [ŋ] 'unha','algunha' (the RAG grapheme for the phonemic velar nasal) per freixeiro_mato1998; palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'mulleres','colleu' per regueira1996.

sentence: A praza da catedral ten doce columnas de pedra.
ipa: ˈa ˈpɾaθa ˈda kateˈdɾal ˈteŋ ˈdoθe koˈlumnas ˈde ˈpedɾa
gloss_en: The cathedral square has twelve columns of stone.
notes: Distinción ⟨za⟩/⟨ce⟩ [θ] 'praza','doce' per regueira1996; word-final velar [ŋ] 'ten' per freixeiro_mato1998.
